_metadata.description: Passage-level retrieval eval slice (T-DOC41, Contextual Retrieval spike): ~40 questions each grounded in one real equation or algorithm/pseudocode block from the corpus, each answerable only by that specific block -- not by surrounding prose or the paper's abstract. Built to detect an equation-chunk retrieval-quality change that fixtures/eval/eval_ground_truth.json's paper-level scoring cannot see (see its _metadata.multi_gold_note and reviews/T-EVAL-REPORT.md secs 2.2-2.3).
_metadata.structure: Array of records keyed by question_id. gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id identify the CHUNK that carries the target equation/algorithm: chunks anchor to the first block of their run-length group (rag/chunker.py:_build_chunk), and an equation/algorithm block is never itself a group's first block in this corpus (verified empirically: 0 of 31,068 equation blocks are ever a chunk anchor) -- so gold_block_id/gold_chunk_id point at the chunk's real anchor block (its introducing prose), while passage_excerpt is the verbatim equation/algorithm text, itself a genuine substring of that chunk's full stored text (chunks concatenate every block in their group). Passage-level Recall@10 scoring (app/retrieval_eval.py) checks a returned result's anchor.block_id against gold_block_id -- equivalently, whether the CHUNK carrying this equation was retrieved, which is the retrieval unit that actually matters.
_metadata.authoring_method: Each source block was chosen from blocks.type='equation' (or, for Algorithm-Retrieval, a prose block whose text matches (?i)^\s*algorithm\s+\d), filtered to substantive length (60-611 chars) from Method/Model/Estimation/Identification/Results-flavored section_paths, read together with its neighboring prose (adjacent idx blocks in the same paper) to understand what it defines, then written as a natural-language question a researcher would ask that only this equation/algorithm answers -- never answerable from the abstract or generic prose. Diversity: 39 distinct source papers across causal inference/ML, econometrics, and biostatistics methodology (one paper contributes both an equation and an algorithm question). Gold-chunk resolution and the empty-anchor-set finding were verified directly against papers.db (read-only), not assumed.

ground_truth.question_text: What is the two-step GMM procedure (Algorithm 4.2) that stacks moment functions from a dictionary of basis functions, used for efficient estimation under spillovers?
ground_truth.source_paper_title: A Design-Based Approach to Testing and Inference in (Quasi-)Experiments with Spillovers
ground_truth.section_path: 4 Eficient Moments
ground_truth.passage_excerpt: Algorithm 4.2 (Two-step GMM). Given a dictionary $\{ ( \phi _ { m } , \psi _ { m } ) : m = 1 , \ldots , M \}$ , stack $\eta _ { n } ^ { ( M ) } ( \theta ) : =$ $\begin{array} { r } { \big ( \eta _ { 1 , n } ( \theta ) , \dots , \eta _ { M , n } ( \theta ) \big ) ^ { \top } , \eta _ { m , n } ( \theta ) : = \frac { 1 } { N _ { n } } \sum _ { i \in \mathcal { U } _ { n } } \phi _ { m } ( Y _ { i } ) R _ { i , \theta } \psi _ { m } ( W ) } \end{array}$

ground_truth.question_text: What two-stage procedure does the Two-Stage alpha-Optimization Algorithm use to pinpoint the optimal intervention strength under the feasibility set, combining a monotonicity-based boundary search with a refinement step?
ground_truth.source_paper_title: Inside the Unfair Judge: A Mechanistic Interpretability Account of LLM-as-Judge Bias
ground_truth.section_path: J.4. Two-Stage �-Optimization Algorithm
ground_truth.passage_excerpt: Algorithm 1 pinpoints the optimal intervention strength under the feasibility set $\mathcal { F }$ defined in Eq. (5). Stage 1 exploits the monotonicity of $V ( \alpha )$ and $\rho _ { S } ( \alpha )$ to locate the boundary $\alpha _ { \mathrm { b d } }$ in $\mathcal { O } ( \log \alpha _ { \mathrm { m a x } } +$ log(1/�)) evaluations. Stage 2 refines by simulated annealing within a small neighborhood of $\alpha _ { \mathrm { b d } }$ which is necessary because the integer-score output makes $W _ { 1 } ( \alpha )$ piecewise-constant and induces local plateaus that a pure greedy search would get stuck on.

ground_truth.question_text: What does Algorithm 1's streaming tracker require after each intervention to support anytime-valid confidence sequences for certified interventional fidelity?
ground_truth.source_paper_title: Certified Interventional Fidelity: Anytime-Valid, Adaptive Evaluation of Causal Claims in Mechanistic Interpretability
ground_truth.section_path: 3 CERTIFIED INTERVENTIONAL FIDELITY > 3.4 VARIANCE-ADAPTIVE CONFIDENCE SEQUENCES VIA BETTING
ground_truth.passage_excerpt: Algorithm 1 only requires a streaming tracker: after each intervention, update with the observed score and optional importance weight, then query lower and upper confidence bounds. Hoeffding and betting CSs therefore serve as interchangeable evaluation layers.
